name: Vía de la Plata: Calzada de Valdunciel to Castle of Good Love
name_original: Castillo de Buen Amor desde Calzada de Valdunciel por la Vía de la Plata
description: Hike 14.6 miles (23.4 km) on the Vía de la Plata from Calzada de Valdunciel to the historic Castle of Good Love, gaining 412 feet (126 metre
region_intro: Hiking around Calzada De Valdunciel offers routes through a predominantly flat landscape, characterized by vast cultivated fields and small forests. The area is traversed by the ancient Vía de la Plata, a historic pilgrimage path that adds cultural significance to the trails. Natural water features like the Arroyo de la Encina and Arroyo de la Vega enhance the serene Castilian environment. Subtle variations in topography are provided by features such as the Cerro de la Septa hill. Best hiking trails around Calzada De Valdunciel The most popular hiking route is Los Molinos Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a gentle exploration of the local countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Santa Lucía Trail Loop, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This loop provides varied scenery through fields and along natural features. Local hikers also love the Vía de la Plata: Calzada de Valdunciel to Castle of Good Love, a 14.6 miles (23.4 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Calzada De Valdunciel is defined by vast cultivated fields, small forests, and the historic Vía de la Plata. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Calzada De Valdunciel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.96 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Calzada De Valdunciel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscape can I expect when hiking around Calzada de Valdunciel?
A: The area around Calzada de Valdunciel features a predominantly flat landscape, characterized by vast cultivated fields and small forests. You'll also encounter natural water features like the Arroyo de la Encina and Arroyo de la Vega, and subtle variations in topography such as the Cerro de la Septa hill. The historic Vía de la Plata also traverses the region, adding a cultural dimension to the natural scenery.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Calzada de Valdunciel?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes available around Calzada de Valdunciel, offering a variety of options for different preferences and skill levels. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 3.96 stars.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Calzada de Valdunciel offers 23 easy hiking routes, perfect for beginners or families looking for a gentle outdoor experience. These trails provide a relaxed way to explore the local countryside. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993174206/los-molinos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Molinos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.8 miles (9.3 km) long and takes about 1 hour 54 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find moderate hiking trails in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a bit more challenge, there are 15 moderate hiking trails around Calzada de Valdunciel. These routes often provide varied scenery through fields and along natural features. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208816730/santa-lucia-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path that offers diverse views.

Q: Are there any circular walks available?
A: Yes, you can find circular walks in Calzada de Valdunciel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208816730/santa-lucia-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that allows you to start and end your hike in the same location while enjoying varied scenery.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Calzada de Valdunciel is rich in history, especially due to its location on the ancient Vía de la Plata. Along the trails, you can encounter ancient Roman constructions. Notable historical features include the Roman funerary stele known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3495885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Buena&lt;/a&gt;, fragments of Roman miliarios (milestones) called Pontones, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt; itself. The Centro de Interpretación de los Caminos Históricos also offers deeper insights into the area's legacy.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Calzada de Valdunciel?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.96 stars from over 15 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil environment, the extensive fields, and the historical significance of walking along the Vía de la Plata. The blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage is a major draw.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Calzada de Valdunciel dog-friendly?
A: While specific dog restrictions are not noted, the rural and open nature of the trails around Calzada de Valdunciel, with vast fields and small forests, generally makes them suitable for hiking with dogs. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations regarding livestock or protected areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Calzada de Valdunciel?
A: Given the region's character as the 'granary of the province' with extensive open fields, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times for hiking. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape offers beautiful colors. Summers can be hot, and winters might be chilly, but hiking is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: For those looking for a longer journey, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993172569/via-de-la-plata-calzada-de-valdunciel-to-castle-of-good-love" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata: Calzada de Valdunciel to Castle of Good Love&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.6 miles (23.4 km) trail. This route takes approximately 4 hours 50 minutes to complete and offers an immersive experience through historical landscapes, connecting Calzada de Valdunciel with the provincial border towards Zamora.

Q: Is there public transport access to the hiking trails in Calzada de Valdunciel?
A: Calzada de Valdunciel is a stage on the Vía de la Plata, making it accessible for those undertaking the Camino de Santiago. While specific public transport links directly to trailheads are not detailed, the village itself can be reached, and many routes start from or pass through the town center, where you can find facilities like the Albergue de Peregrinos.
tips: This section of the Vía de la Plata runs through the Castilian plateau and is a perfect gravel track with beautiful scenery.

Originally known as Castillo de Villanueva Cañedo, it has its origins in the 11th century, being built as part of an imposing military fortress.
In the 15th century it passed into the hands of the Catholic Monarchs who used it as supplies for the troops in the War of Succession.
After a period of splendor as the residence of Alonso de Fonseca, the castle was abandoned until it was rescued for agricultural use until the 19th century. It is in the 20th century that it is completely restored to return it to its former splendor and today it is a recognized inn of excellent level.

The parish church of Santa Elena was built in the 16th century, although the main portal, the portico with its large carved scallop and the transparent alcove of the main chapel were redone around 1728.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this section of the Vía de la Plata?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike from Calzada de Valdunciel to the Castle of Good Love is estimated to take around 4 hours and 50 minutes to complete. This duration is for hiking and does not include stops for rest or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of a "perfect gravel track" for much of the way, offering smooth, flat, and ochre-colored paths. You'll traverse the Castilian plateau, with sections that may head down into a valley alongside the Encina stream. The landscape features extensive fields of wheat, corn, and sunflowers, characteristic of the La Armuña farming area.

faqs.question: Is this section of the Vía de la Plata suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the paths are described as "easily-accessible" and generally suitable for "all skill levels" in terms of navigability, the overall hike is classified as moderate due to its length of over 23 kilometers. Hikers should have a decent level of physical fitness to comfortably complete the route.

faqs.question: What historical and natural landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be following a segment of the ancient Vía de la Plata - Camino de Santiago, a historic Roman road. Key highlights include the impressive Castle of Good Love at the end, and the Church of Santa Elena of Calzada de Valdunciel near the start. The route also offers views of the Castilian plateau with its vast agricultural fields and varied local flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the larger Vía de la Plata - Camino de Santiago. It also shares sections with the Mtb Vía de la Plata (77% of the route), Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga (77%), and the Cañada Real de La Vizana o de la Plata.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses an agricultural region with "big skies, big horizons." Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in this region, offering milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring colder conditions, though the paths remain accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Vía de la Plata: Calzada de Valdunciel to Castle of Good Love trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact segment are not detailed, the Vía de la Plata is a long-distance route. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in Spain, especially in rural areas, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this section of the Vía de la Plata. It is a public trail. However, if you plan to visit the Castle of Good Love, which operates as a hotel, there might be fees for specific services or access to certain areas within the castle grounds.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this hike in Calzada de Valdunciel?
faqs.answer: While specific parking lots are not detailed, Calzada de Valdunciel is a small village. You can typically find parking near the Church of Santa Elena or other central points in the village, which serves as the starting point for this section of the Vía de la Plata.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail's end at the Castle of Good Love?
faqs.answer: The Castle of Good Love itself operates as a luxury hotel and likely offers dining facilities for its guests. For more general public options, you might need to check nearby villages or towns, as the castle is nestled within a large meadow, offering a tranquil natural setting away from immediate urban amenities.
